config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Society matters a lot, but I do not think its interests should automatically come before individual rights, because that can easily become an excuse for abuse by governments or majorities. In my view, individual rights are the boundary that keeps social goals from turning into oppression. A healthy society should protect both, but when they clash, rights should not be treated as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society’s interests should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in China, people often understand why collective stability and public order matter, but I have also seen how easily “the greater good” can be used to silence ordinary people or justify unfair treatment. Individual rights are not an obstacle to society; they are a safeguard that keeps social goals from becoming arbitrary power. A strong society should protect the common good without treating personal rights as something that can be discarded whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society’s interests should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an Indonesian perspective, we do value gotong royong and social harmony, but I have also seen how “for the public good” can be used to excuse corruption, intimidation, or pressure to stay silent. Rights are not a selfish luxury; they are the limit that prevents the majority or the state from trampling ordinary people. Society is strongest when it protects people first and then builds the common good on that foundation.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my perspective in China, people do value stability, order, and collective welfare, but that does not mean the individual can be treated as expendable whenever leaders claim a social benefit. I have seen how easily “for the common good” can become a justification for pressure, silence, or unfair treatment, so rights need real protection, not just promises. Society is healthier when it serves people without erasing them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. In Indonesia, we value gotong royong and harmony, but I have also seen how appeals to the common good can be abused to pressure people into silence or let those in power avoid accountability. For me, rights are not anti-social; they are what keep society from becoming a tool of the strongest group. A good society should balance both, but when forced to choose, individual rights should not be treated as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my perspective in China, people do value stability and collective welfare, but I have also seen how easily “for the common good” can become a way to pressure ordinary people and excuse unfair treatment. Society does need shared rules and public order, yet those goals become dangerous when they are used to erase basic rights or silence dissent. A strong society should protect the common good while still treating individual rights as real limits, not optional privileges.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
